Cross-border workers should carefully plan their trips, taking into account the new fares and available transport options. They can use the [public transport cost calculator](nav:calculator) to calculate the costs of public transport and find the most convenient routes.

### 4. Use mobility vouchers

If the initiative is approved, cross-border workers could receive mobility vouchers worth approximately 110 francs per year. These vouchers could be used for train journeys and other public transport. Cross-border workers should find out how to use the mobility vouchers and plan their trips accordingly.

### 5. Monitor news

Cross-border workers should monitor news related to the initiative and any potential changes in public transport and airline regulations. They can consult the websites of local authorities and involved organizations to stay updated.

### Useful tools

To plan their trips and calculate the costs of public transport, cross-border workers can use the [public transport cost calculator](nav:calculator). Additionally, they can consult the [public transport map](nav:border-map) to find the most convenient and accessible routes.

### Conclusion

The popular initiative to tax airline tickets and promote public transport could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. Cross-border workers should get informed about the initiative, sign the petition, plan their trips, and use mobility vouchers to take advantage of the potential benefits. Monitoring news and staying updated on regulatory changes is crucial to making the most of the new transport opportunities.

item.title: Airpack Lombardia: 41 jobs at risk of dismissal
item.description: Belgian owner Abriso insists on closing the Ossago Lodigiano plant, despite purchase offers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 41 workers at risk of dismissal
- Belgian owner Abriso insists on closure
- Decisive meeting on April 30 with the CEO

## Key facts
- **What**: Closure of the Airpack plant
- When: Final deadline May 3, 2026
- Where: Ossago Lodigiano, Lombardy
- Who: Belgian owner Abriso, unions Filctem Cgil and Femca Cisl
- Amount: 41 workers involved

New stalemate in the Airpack dispute in Ossago Lodigiano. Despite the interest of new buyers, the Belgian owner Abriso does not yield on the closure: the final deadline for the 41 workers is May 3. The last meeting held yesterday at the Pirellone between the unions (Filctem Cgil and Femca Cisl) and the representatives of the multinational Abriso ended with a disheartening nothing done. The owner reiterated the intention to proceed with the closure of the plant, showing extreme rigidity: to date, the company refuses to resort to extraordinary wage guarantee fund, a tool that would allow freezing layoffs and evaluating the two expressions of interest already received from other entrepreneurs.

### The unions' position
"The company has shown itself closed to all our proposals – explains Morwenna Di Benedetto (Cgil) – It does not evaluate the wage guarantee fund nor the time necessary to analyze the purchase offers for part of the production." Moreover, despite the unions' requests, at the table in Lombardy Region once again the CEO of the company did not show up, who sent only the plant director and their lawyer, fundamentally without real negotiating power.

### The unions' requests and the uncertainty of May 3
The climate is one of extreme uncertainty. The collective dismissal procedure will expire on May 3: without an agreement or a suspension, the employees will face unemployment. The union secretaries, including Stefano Priori (Cisl), emphasize how the wage guarantee fund would serve to:
- Bridge the production site to the new management.
- Relieve the company of direct personnel costs during the sale negotiation.
- Provide economic breathing room for the 41 families involved.

### The last hope
April 30, the last call: the CEO is expected at the table. All hopes are now placed on the meeting scheduled for Thursday, April 30. For the first time, the participation of the CEO of Airpack, so far a great absentee in the institutional confrontation, is expected at the negotiating table. His presence is considered by the unions the last opportunity to unlock the negotiating mandate and move from words to deeds, avoiding the definitive closure of a historic site for the Lodigiano area. ### Implications for Workers
The current situation is critical for the 41 workers involved. Without the extraordinary wage guarantee scheme, employees find themselves in a position of extreme vulnerability. The closure of the plant would not only mean the loss of their jobs but also have a significant impact on their families and the local community.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before this crisis, the Ossago Lodigiano plant was a cornerstone of the local economy. The company's production significantly contributed to the area's economy, offering stable and well-paid jobs. The closure of the plant would be a hard blow to the community, with possible repercussions on the economic and social fabric.

### Possible Scenarios
If the Belgian owner Abriso insists on closure, the workers would face a period of unemployment. The Wage Compensation Scheme (CIG) would be a fundamental tool to mitigate the economic impact, allowing workers to have an income during the transition to new job opportunities. However, without the CIG, workers could face immediate economic difficulties.

### The Importance of the CEO's Presence
The presence of the CEO at the negotiation table is considered crucial by the unions. His participation could represent the last opportunity to find an agreement that avoids the closure of the plant. The unions hope that the CEO can bring new perspectives and concrete solutions to save the jobs.

### Comparisons with Similar Situations
In other similar situations, the extraordinary wage guarantee scheme has helped avoid mass layoffs and find alternative solutions for production continuity. The example of other companies that have used the CIG to transition workers to new management could be a model to follow in this case as well.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do now
The affected workers must stay informed and actively participate in the negotiations. It is crucial that the unions continue to represent their interests and seek concrete solutions to avoid the closure of the plant.

### Procedure to request CIG
If the CIG is approved, workers should follow the established procedures to access the benefits. This may include submitting specific documentation and attending meetings with union and company representatives.

### Economic and psychological support
During this period of uncertainty, it is important that workers receive not only economic but also psychological support. Unions and local organizations might offer counseling and assistance services to help workers manage stress and anxiety related to the situation.

### Useful tools
For further information and support, workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate their options and plan for the future. Additionally, they can consult the guides and resources available on the website to better understand their rights and the procedures to follow.

### Conclusion
The current situation is complex and requires a joint effort from all parties involved. With the active participation of the workers, the support of the unions, and the willingness of the management, it is possible to find a solution that avoids the closure of the plant and ensures a secure future for the 41 workers involved.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/in-5-giorni-si-decide-il-destino-di-41-lavoratori-in-lombardia-il-capo-dellazienda-non-si-vede-mai/)*

item.title: Insubria University: €4.4m for Como and Varese research
item.description: Lombardy Region funds IMPACTT and SustAA projects to boost research and sustainable innovation infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Università Insubria receives 4.4 million from Regione Lombardia
- Funding for IMPACTT and SustAA projects
- Infrastructure enhancement in Como, Varese, and Busto Arsizio

## Key facts
- **What**: Funding for research infrastructure
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Como, Varese, Busto Arsizio
- Who: Università Insubria, Regione Lombardia
- Amount: 4,400,000 euros

The Università dell'Insubria in Como and Varese has received a 4.4 million euro funding from Regione Lombardia to enhance research infrastructure. The funding, awarded through a regional tender, will be used to develop new technological platforms and strengthen existing ones at the Como, Varese, and Busto Arsizio campuses. The funding is part of the overall 50 million euro allocation of the Regional Fesr Program 2021-2027, exclusively destined for public and private universities in Lombardy for the creation and modernization of research infrastructure.

### Funded projects
The funding will be managed by Criett, the Research and Technological Transfer Center of Università dell'Insubria, founded in 2024. Criett has secured resources for two strategic projects: IMPACTT and SustAA. The first project, IMPACTT, receives nearly 3 million euros (2,999,860.77 euros) and aims to enhance the Material Analysis and Characterization Platform in Como and establish a new biomedical research platform in Busto Arsizio. The goal is to valorize research and technological transfer in the sectors of advanced materials and diagnostics for aging, within the context of the Silver Economy. The second project, SustAA, receives approximately 1.4 million euros (1,414,454.40 euros) and involves the creation in Varese of an infrastructure dedicated to sustainable innovation in the sector of amino acids and their derivatives. The initiative is part of the 'Health and Life Science' ecosystem and supports the territory's vocation in the nutraceutical, pharmaceutical, cosmetic, and food sectors.

### Objectives and impact
According to regional assessor Alessandro Fermi, the objective is to strengthen the dialogue between universities and businesses, particularly small and medium-sized enterprises, and transform research into a driver of development, innovation, and competitiveness for the territory. Professor Flavia Marinelli, scientific director of Criett, vice-director professor Mauro Fasano, professors Sandro Recchia and Tiziana Rubino, referents of the IMPACTT project, and professor Loredano Pollegioni, proposer of SustAA, together with the Scientific Council of Criett, are already at work to define the next operational phases of the projects and organize a moment of exchange with the scientific community of the university dedicated to the use of the new funds. - ## Implications for the territory
The 4.4 million euro funding represents a significant opportunity for the Como, Varese, and Busto Arsizio area. Enhancing research infrastructures will not only improve the quality of university education but also contribute to creating new collaborations between the academic world and local businesses. This dialogue is fundamental to transforming research into concrete innovation, which can lead to new products, services, and technologies.

### Collaboration with businesses
One of the main objectives of the funding is to strengthen the link between universities and businesses, particularly small and medium-sized enterprises. This type of collaboration is essential to transfer the knowledge gained through research into practical solutions that can be implemented in the market. For example, the IMPACTT project aims to create a direct connection between local businesses and international research centers, facilitating the exchange of ideas and technologies.

### Economic impact
Investing in research infrastructures can have a significant economic impact on the territory. By attracting high-profile projects and talents, the University of Insubria can contribute to creating new jobs and stimulating local economic growth. Furthermore, enhancing research infrastructures can attract external investments, further strengthening the region's economy.

### Sustainable innovation
The SustAA project, dedicated to sustainable innovation in the amino acids and their derivatives sector, is an example of how research can contribute to developing sustainable solutions for key sectors such as nutraceuticals, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and food. This type of research not only promotes technological innovation but also contributes to creating a more sustainable and healthier environment for citizens.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). - ## Next Steps
Now that the funding has been allocated, the University of Insubria is working to define the next operational phases of the IMPACTT and SustAA projects. This includes detailed planning of research activities, purchasing new equipment, and creating new collaborations with local businesses. Professor Flavia Marinelli and her team are organizing a meeting with the university's scientific community to discuss the use of the new funds and ensure they are used effectively.

### What Businesses Can Do
Small and medium-sized enterprises interested in collaborating with the University of Insubria can contact Criett to explore collaboration opportunities. This can include participation in joint research projects, access to new technologies, and the use of research infrastructures to develop new products and services. Businesses can also benefit from technology transfer, which can help them stay competitive in a constantly evolving market.

### Opportunities for Researchers
Researchers interested in participating in the IMPACTT and SustAA projects can contact Criett for information on how to join the research team. This is an excellent opportunity for researchers to work on innovative projects and collaborate with top-level experts. Additionally, researchers can benefit from the new research infrastructures, which offer state-of-the-art equipment and a stimulating work environment.

### Useful Tools
For more information on the IMPACTT and SustAA projects and how to participate, visit the Criett website or contact the University of Insubria directly. Additionally, to calculate the economic impact of these projects on the territory, you can use the [economic calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/universita-dellinsubria-como-e-varese-in-festa-da-regione-lombardia-arrivano-44-milioni-di-euro/)*

item.title: Electric cars: charging is the most critical moment
item.description: A fire in a Breganzona garage has refocused attention on the risks associated with charging electric cars.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Fire in a garage in Breganzona, probably caused by an electric car while charging
- The charging moment is the most critical for electric cars
- Claudio Dick: "In 90% of cases, the most delicate moment is charging"

## Key facts
- **What**: Fire in a garage in Breganzona
- When: Last Friday (unspecified date)
- Where: Garage of a building in Breganzona
- Who: Swiss Firefighters Federation, Claudio Dick
- Amount: Not specified

A car caught fire last Friday in a garage of a building in Breganzona, damaging other vehicles and the apartments above. The fire likely started from an electric car during battery charging. According to the Swiss Firefighters Federation, electric cars are not more flammable than gasoline or diesel ones. However, these vehicles are often charged in garages, which could be the most critical moment.

Claudio Dick, an expert in electric motors and batteries, explains that "in 90% of cases, the most delicate moment is always charging. We input current and create a chemical process that, together with voltage, charges the battery. Here, what happened in Breganzona could occur."

### Safety of electric cars

Although data indicates that electric cars, while driving, can be even safer than internal combustion engine cars, attention focuses on the charging phase. Claudio Dick emphasizes that "I wouldn't say we should worry. Maybe we should do some prevention on fire safety systems that can be installed."

The evolution of electric cars in the last 10 years has been exponential, and there are still many improvements to be made. Soon, a solid-state battery will also arrive on the market, which will be even safer.

### Precautions for charging

Regarding the precautions to be taken in your own garages, Dick warns that "it is useful to have, in the first minutes, fire extinguishers near the charging points. They must be specific extinguishers for lithium batteries and not just any extinguisher. And obviously, do not use water."

This incident brings back the spotlight on the risks related to charging electric cars and the importance of adopting adequate preventive measures. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The incident in Breganzona raises important questions for cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy, many of whom might consider buying an electric car to reduce fuel costs and benefit from Swiss tax incentives.

### Comparison between electric and traditional cars

Electric cars are often considered safer than gasoline or diesel cars when in motion. However, the charging phase presents specific risks that need to be managed carefully. This is particularly relevant for cross-border workers who might need to charge their electric cars in condominium garages or public parking lots.

### Preventive measures

For cross-border workers who own or are considering buying an electric car, it is fundamental to adopt adequate preventive measures. This includes installing specific extinguishers for lithium batteries near charging points. Additionally, it is important to follow the guidelines provided by electric car manufacturers and firefighters to ensure maximum safety during charging.

### Tax incentives

The Canton of Ticino offers several tax incentives for the purchase of electric cars, which can be particularly advantageous for cross-border workers. These incentives may include reductions in circulation taxes and subsidies for the installation of domestic charging points. However, it is important to verify the specific requirements and conditions to access these incentives.

### Future scenarios

With the arrival on the market of solid-state batteries, which will be even safer, the risks related to charging electric cars could decrease. This could make electric cars an even more attractive option for cross-border workers seeking to reduce transportation costs and benefit from tax incentives.

### Conclusion

The incident in Breganzona serves as a reminder of the importance of adopting adequate preventive measures when owning an electric car. For cross-border workers, this means not only benefiting from tax incentives but also ensuring safety during charging. With the right precautions, electric cars can be a safe and convenient option for cross-border workers living and working between Ticino and Italy.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to ensure safety during charging

For cross-border workers who own an electric car or are considering buying one, it is essential to adopt adequate preventive measures to ensure safety during charging. Here are some concrete steps that can be followed:

### Installation of specific fire extinguishers

1. **Purchase of specific fire extinguishers**: Make sure to buy fire extinguishers specifically designed for lithium batteries. These extinguishers are designed to handle fires involving lithium batteries and are more effective than traditional extinguishers.
2. Placement of fire extinguishers: Place the fire extinguishers near the charging points, so that they are easily accessible in case of emergency.
3. Training on the use of fire extinguishers: Make sure you know how to use the fire extinguishers correctly and inform any other users of the garage on how to use them if necessary.

### Follow the manufacturers' guidelines

1. Instruction manuals: Read carefully the instruction manuals provided with the electric car and with the charging system. These manuals contain important information on safety and the correct procedures for charging.
2. Software updates: Make sure to keep the software of the electric car updated. Updates may include security improvements and bug fixes that could affect safety during charging.
3. Technical assistance: In case of doubts or problems, contact the customer service of the manufacturer of the electric car or the charging system to obtain technical assistance.

### Check tax incentives

1. Information on incentives: Check the tax incentives available for the purchase of electric cars and for the installation of domestic charging points. These incentives may include reductions in circulation taxes and subsidies for installation.
2. Necessary documentation: Make sure you have all the necessary documentation to access tax incentives. This may include purchase invoices, installation receipts and compliance certificates.
3. Deadlines: Pay attention to the deadlines for submitting tax incentive applications. Make sure to submit the necessary documentation within the established deadlines.

### Use useful tools

For further information and assistance, cross-border workers can use tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare the costs of an electric car with a traditional one. Additionally, they can consult the guides and resources available on the site to obtain detailed information on tax incentives and preventive measures.

### Conclusion

Adopting adequate preventive measures is fundamental to ensure safety during the charging of electric cars. By following the steps described and using the available tools, cross-border workers can benefit from tax incentives and enjoy greater safety during the charging of their electric cars.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3693849)*

item.title: Como Municipality: 12 counters by appointment only from May 4, 2026
item.description: From May 4, 2026, 12 services of the Como Municipality will be available by appointment only, including the issuance of the Electronic Identity Card (CIE).
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- From May 4, 2026, 12 services of the Municipality of Como will be by appointment only
- 60 weekly tickets for the release without appointment of the CIE
- Appointments bookable only for the month of May

## Key facts
- **What**: Municipal services by appointment
- When: From May 4, 2026
- Where: Municipality of Como
- Who: Municipality of Como
- Amount: 60 weekly tickets for CIE without appointment

The Municipality of Como has announced that, starting from Monday, May 4, 2026, twelve services will be provided by appointment only. Among these, the service for issuing the Electronic Identity Card (CIE), certifications, transfer of vehicle ownership, authentication of signatures and copies of documents, substitute declarations, civil status certificates, historical civil status certificates, issuance of electoral certifications, issuance of new electoral card, issuance of duplicate electoral card, registration/cancellation of the list of scrutineers and presidents of polling stations. Appointments can be booked only for the month of May, while dates for subsequent months will be made available by May.

For the issuance of the CIE, only for the month of May, up to 60 tickets will be available for the release without appointment every Monday from 15:30 to 19:30, exclusively for residents or inhabitants who need to replace the Paper Identity Card with the CIE. Furthermore, it is possible to obtain the issuance of the CIE for urgent reasons, duly documented or self-certified, such as travel, health, participation in public tenders/competitions, theft or loss, enrollment in schools, universities or other courses, and change of personal details.

Users falling within the above-mentioned cases will be received only at the end of the scheduled appointments and will receive an appointment within the next 3 days. The renewal of the document can be requested starting from the 180th day prior to the expiry date. For special cases and by appointment, it is possible to request the home service offered by the civic centers of Sagnino and Albate.

To book an appointment and for any further information, please refer to the official website of the Municipality of Como. It is recommended to carefully read the instructions reported on the website and the vademecum for the issuance of the CIE in order to have the necessary documents for the issuance of the CIE.

### Procedure for the issuance of the CIE
To request the CIE, it is necessary to follow a specific procedure. First of all, it is important to verify the documents required for the issuance of the CIE, which can be consulted on the official website of the Municipality of Como. Once the documents have been collected, it is possible to book an appointment through the official website. During the appointment, it will be necessary to present the required documents and follow the instructions provided by the registry office.

For citizens who need to replace the Paper Identity Card with the CIE, it is possible to take advantage of the 60 weekly tickets available every Monday from 15:30 to 19:30. This service is reserved for residents or inhabitants in the Municipality of Como. For those who need the issuance of the CIE for urgent reasons, it is possible to directly contact the Registry Office, upon documentation or self-certification of the reasons.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers residing in the municipality of Como and working in Switzerland may be affected by these changes. It is important that cross-border workers verify the necessary documents for the issuance of the CIE and book an appointment well in advance to avoid delays. Additionally, it is crucial to consider the deadlines and requirements for renewing the document, especially for those who need the CIE for work or travel purposes.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the introduction of the appointment system, municipal services were available on a walk-in basis, which could result in prolonged waits and queues. With the new system, citizens will benefit from more efficient time management and reduced waiting times. However, it is important that citizens plan ahead and book appointments in advance, especially for high-demand services such as the issuance of the CIE.

### Concrete procedures
To schedule an appointment, you need to access the official website of the Municipality of Como and follow the instructions reported in the section dedicated to appointments. It is important to carefully read the instructions and the vademecum for the issuance of the CIE to ensure you have all the necessary documents. In case of doubts or need for further information, you can contact the Anagrafe Office of the Municipality of Como.

For citizens who require home services, it is possible to request an appointment by contacting the civic centers of Sagnino and Albate. This service is reserved for special cases and subject to availability. ### Practical Implications for Citizens
The introduction of the appointment system for municipal services will have several practical implications for citizens. First of all, it will be necessary to plan ahead for visits to municipal offices, especially for the most requested services such as the issuance of the CIE. This could mean more organization for citizens, but also a reduction in waiting times and more efficient time management.

### Comparisons with Other Municipalities
The Municipality of Como is not the first to introduce an appointment system for municipal services. Other Italian municipalities have already adopted this system to improve efficiency and reduce waiting times. However, it is important that citizens inform themselves about the specific procedures of their own municipality and organize accordingly.

### Concrete Scenarios
Let's imagine a citizen who needs to renew their CIE for work reasons. This citizen will have to book an appointment through the official website of the Municipality of Como and go to the registry office with the necessary documents. If the citizen needs an urgent appointment, they can contact the Registry Office directly, with prior documentation or self-certification of the reasons.

Another scenario could involve a citizen who needs to authenticate a signature or a copy of a document. In this case as well, it will be necessary to book an appointment through the official website and go to the municipal office with the required documents. If the citizen needs a home service, they can request an appointment after contacting the Sagnino and Albate civic centers.

### Step-by-Step Procedures
1. Check the documents required for the requested service on the official website of the Municipality of Como.
2. Book an appointment through the official website, following the instructions reported in the section dedicated to appointments.
3. Go to the municipal office on the day and at the time of the appointment with the necessary documents.
4. Follow the instructions provided by the municipal office to complete the procedure.
5. In case of need for a home service, contact the Sagnino and Albate civic centers to request an appointment.

### Useful Tools
To help citizens navigate the new appointment system, the Municipality of Como has made a series of useful tools available on its official website. Among these, a vademecum for the issuance of the CIE, a section dedicated to appointments with detailed instructions, and a contact page for the Registry Office.

### Conclusion
The introduction of the appointment system for municipal services represents an important step towards more efficient and organized management of the services offered by the Municipality of Como. However, it is fundamental that citizens inform themselves about the new procedures and organize accordingly to avoid delays and inconveniences.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for citizens
To best address the new procedures introduced by the Municipality of Como, it is important for citizens to follow some concrete actions. First and foremost, it is essential to verify the necessary documents for the requested service on the official website of the Municipality of Como. This step is crucial to ensure that all the necessary materials are available and to avoid delays during the appointment.

### Appointment booking
Once the documents have been verified, it is possible to book an appointment through the official website of the Municipality of Como. It is important to carefully follow the instructions reported in the section dedicated to appointments and to ensure that all the required data is entered correctly. In case of doubts or difficulties, it is possible to contact the Registry Office for assistance.

### Presentation at the appointment
On the day and time of the appointment, it is important to present yourself at the municipal office with the necessary documents. It is advisable to arrive a few minutes early to avoid any delays and to ensure that all the required materials are available. During the appointment, it is essential to follow the instructions provided by the municipal office to complete the procedure in the most efficient way possible.

### Home service
For citizens who need a home service, it is possible to request an appointment by contacting the civic centers of Sagnino and Albate. This service is reserved for special cases and subject to availability. It is important to contact the civic centers in advance to check availability and arrange an appointment.

### Use of online tools
The Municipality of Como has made a series of useful tools available on its official website to help citizens navigate the new appointment system. Among these, a vademecum for the issuance of the CIE, a section dedicated to appointments with detailed instructions and a contact page for the Registry Office. It is advisable to consult these tools to ensure that all the necessary information is available and to complete the procedures in the most efficient way possible.

### Conclusion
The introduction of the appointment system for municipal services represents an important step towards a more efficient and organized management of the services offered by the Municipality of Como. However, it is essential that citizens inform themselves about the new procedures and organize accordingly to avoid delays and inconveniences. By following the concrete actions described in this article, citizens will be able to best address the new challenges and take advantage of municipal services in an efficient and stress-free manner.

For further information and to book an appointment, visit the official website of the Municipality of Como [here](nav:calculator).

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/comune-di-como-per-12-sportelli-il-servizio-diventa-solo-su-appuntamento-cosa-cambia-a-maggio-per-il-rilascio-della-cie/)*

item.title: Tax on planes to fund trains: initiative in Ticino
item.description: A committee proposes taxing plane tickets to support public transport in Switzerland, with a minimum contribution of 30 francs.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Minimum tax of 30 francs per airline ticket
- Expected revenue: 1.5 billion francs per year
- Two-thirds of funds in public transport vouchers

## Key facts
- **What**: Initiative to tax airline tickets
- When: Launched on October 15, 2024
- Where: Switzerland, with direct impact in Ticino
- Who: Comitato actif-trafiC
- Amount: 1.5 billion francs per year
- Goal: Support public transport

A committee aims to increase the cost of airline tickets to support passenger rail transport in Switzerland. The initiative, launched on Tuesday, provides for a minimum contribution of 30 francs per airline ticket, proportional to the distance and class of flight. According to the committee, the expected revenue from this tax amounts to approximately 1.5 billion francs per year. At least two-thirds of this amount will be paid to the population in the form of vouchers for public transport. The rest of the revenue will allow the development of rail links to neighboring countries.

Martine Docourt, National Councilor (PS/NE), emphasizes that the initiative will bring financial benefits to approximately 90% of the population. The vast majority of the population will receive more money in vouchers than they will spend in taxes.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who frequently travel by plane could be affected by this tax. However, the vouchers for public transport could compensate for the additional costs, making train travel more convenient for commuters.

### Concrete examples
- Current cost: A flight ticket from Lugano to Zurich costs approximately 150 francs. With the new tax, the cost would increase to 180 francs.
- Public transport vouchers: A cross-border worker who travels once a month from Chiasso to Milan would spend approximately 60 francs in annual taxes but would receive vouchers worth 120 francs.

### Regulations and dates
- Effective date: January 1, 2026
- Tax amount: Minimum of 30 francs, up to 120 francs for first-class intercontinental flights.

### Operational checklist
1. Tax calculation: Verify the distance and class of flight.
2. Distribution of vouchers: Vouchers will be distributed quarterly.
3. Use of vouchers: Vouchers can be used on all public transport in Switzerland.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker who travels by plane from Mendrisio to Milan once a month will spend 60 francs in annual taxes but will receive vouchers worth 120 francs.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker who uses the train from Bellinzona to Lugano every day will spend approximately 1,200 francs per year on a subscription but will receive vouchers worth 240 francs.

### Conclusion
The initiative represents a significant step towards the sustainability of public transport in Switzerland, with a direct impact on cross-border workers in Ticino. The vouchers for public transport could compensate for the additional costs, making train travel more convenient for commuters. The initiative aims to make public transport more accessible and attractive, especially for cross-border commuters who cross the Italian-Swiss border. Public transport vouchers could be used for train travel both in Switzerland and neighboring countries, thus facilitating cross-border mobility. For example, a cross-border worker who travels daily from Varese to Lugano could benefit from a voucher worth 500 Swiss francs per year, significantly reducing travel costs.

### Comparison with the current situation
Currently, cross-border workers who use the plane for frequent travel could benefit from public transport vouchers, reducing travel costs. However, it is important to consider that the introduction of a tax on airline tickets, scheduled for 2025, could increase costs for those who frequently travel by plane. For example, a flight from Milan to Zurich could see an increase of approximately 20 Swiss francs per leg.

### Future Scenarios
If the initiative is approved, cross-border workers could see an increase in air travel costs, but also an improvement in railway services. This could make the train a more convenient and attractive option for commuters crossing the border. For example, a train journey from Bellinzona to Locarno could become more affordable thanks to the vouchers, with an estimated 30% savings on tickets.

### Comparison between plane and train

| **Aspect** | Plane | Train |
|------------------------|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Cost | Potentially higher with the tax | Potentially lower with the vouchers |
| Travel time | Faster for long distances | Slower but comfortable for short distances |
| Comfort | Limited for cross-border workers | Greater for commuters |

### Procedure to obtain the vouchers
1. Purchase of an airline ticket subject to the tax.
2. Receipt of a public transport voucher proportional to the tax paid.
3. Use of the voucher for train travel in Switzerland and neighboring countries.

This procedure could simplify access to public transport for cross-border workers, making it more convenient to use the train for their daily commutes. For example, a cross-border worker traveling from Mendrisio to Chiasso could use the voucher to purchase monthly tickets, saving up to 100 Swiss francs per month.

### Operational Checklist
- Verify the compatibility of the airline ticket with the tax.
- Calculate the voucher amount based on the tax paid.
- Use the voucher to purchase railway tickets in Switzerland and neighboring countries.
- Monitor the savings obtained thanks to the vouchers.

### Concrete Examples
- Cross-border worker from Como to Lugano: With a voucher of 600 Swiss francs per year, they could save up to 25% on railway tickets.
- Cross-border worker from Campione d'Italia to Lugano: With a voucher of 400 Swiss francs per year, they could save up to 20% on railway tickets.

### Regulations and Dates
- Introduction date of the tax on airline tickets: January 1, 2025.
- Tax amount: 20 Swiss francs per leg.
- Voucher value: Proportional to the tax paid, with a maximum of 600 Swiss francs per year.

This initiative could represent a significant step towards more sustainable and convenient mobility for cross-border workers in Ticino.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Tax on planes to finance trains: the initiative in Ticino

For cross-border workers who want to take advantage of public transport vouchers, it is crucial to monitor the evolution of the popular initiative 'For public transport, not for private planes', submitted in Ticino in 2023. If the initiative is approved, cross-border workers could benefit from an improvement in railway services and a reduction in travel costs. Currently, the tax on airline tickets is set at 10% of the ticket price, with a minimum amount of 30 CHF and a maximum of 100 CHF. The proceeds, estimated at around 5 million CHF per year, would be used to enhance regional lines, such as Lugano-Ponte Tresa or Bellinzona-Luino.

### Concrete steps
1. **Inform yourself**: Monitor the news on the approval of the initiative, consulting the website of the Ticino Grand Council or local sources such as the Corriere del Ticino.
2. Plan: Consider using the train for frequent trips between towns such as Chiasso and Como, or Lugano and Varese. For example, a plane ticket from Milan to Zurich costs around 150 CHF, subject to a tax of 30 CHF. With this tax, you could get a 30% voucher for public transport, reducing the cost of a monthly subscription from Chiasso to Zurich from 300 CHF to 210 CHF.
3. Use the vouchers: Buy airline tickets subject to the tax to receive vouchers for public transport. For example, a flight from Lugano to Geneva costs around 200 CHF, with a tax of 30 CHF. This voucher could cover 30% of a monthly subscription for the Lugano-Bellinzona line, reducing the cost from 200 CHF to 140 CHF.
4. Save: Use the vouchers for train trips in Switzerland and neighboring countries. For example, a 30 CHF voucher could cover 50% of a single ticket from Lugano to Milan, reducing the cost from 60 CHF to 30 CHF.

### Operational checklist
- [ ] Check the approval of the initiative on the website of the Ticino Grand Council.
- [ ] Calculate the tax on airline tickets using the cost of living calculator.
- [ ] Compare the costs of airline and train tickets between the main Ticino hubs.
- [ ] Plan train trips using the vouchers obtained.

### Useful tools
To calculate the costs and benefits of public transport vouchers, you can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living). This tool helps to compare travel costs and plan movements in the best way. For example, a cross-border worker traveling from Mendrisio to Milan could save around 100 CHF per month using the vouchers.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
- Current scenario: A plane ticket from Lugano to Zurich costs 150 CHF, with a tax of 30 CHF. A monthly subscription for the Lugano-Zurich line costs 300 CHF.
- Scenario with vouchers: With a 30% voucher, the monthly subscription would cost 210 CHF, saving 90 CHF per year.

### Conclusion
The initiative to tax airline tickets and support public transport could have a significant impact on cross-border workers. Monitoring the evolution of the initiative and using the available tools to plan your movements is fundamental to take advantage of the potential benefits. For example, a cross-border worker traveling from Chiasso to Zurich could save around 120 CHF per year using the vouchers.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3698553)*

item.title: Trenord tickets on WhatsApp: how it works
item.description: Trenord introduces tickets purchasable via WhatsApp, available at self-service and Digital Gate in Milan and Malpensa T1
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Trenord tickets purchasable via WhatsApp
- Available at self-service machines and Digital Gates
- Valid for regional, suburban, and cross-border trains

## Key facts
- **What**: Trenord tickets purchasable via WhatsApp
- When: Available from April 2026
- Where: Self-service machines and Digital Gates at Milano Centrale, Milano Cadorna, Milano Porta Garibaldi, and Malpensa T1
- Who: Trenord
- Amount: Not specified

Trenord has introduced a new service that allows you to purchase train tickets directly via WhatsApp. This service is available at the self-service machines and Digital Gates located at the stations of Milano Centrale, Milano Cadorna, Milano Porta Garibaldi, and Malpensa T1. Tickets purchased via WhatsApp are valid for regional and suburban services, the Malpensa Express airport link, and cross-border lines. Additionally, you can purchase integrated tickets for travel in the STIBM area and tickets for 'Train Trips', train+experience packages for leisure time.

### How it works
Tickets on WhatsApp have all the features of a digital ticket: convenient, practical, and purchasable with just a few clicks. The ticket arrives directly via chat: just open the conversation to view all the travel ticket information and show it during the inspection. Railway, airport, and cross-border tickets do not require validation but are activated at the time indicated at purchase. If you depart early, you can advance the time using a dedicated function. For STIBM tickets, activation is required with a 'tap' before departure.

### Purchase method
To receive tickets via WhatsApp, you need to select the WhatsApp option and scan the QR Code on the self-service machine or Digital Gate screen. A pre-filled message will be generated in the 'Trenord Tickets' chat. You will need to confirm the last digits of your mobile number to receive the link to view the ticket and consult all the details.

### Useful information
For those who want to receive train information via WhatsApp as well, the Trenord channel 'My Train' is active. Accessible from a specific link, it allows you to search for your train of interest by entering the train number, indicating stations and times of your journey, or finding it via geolocation. In a few seconds, the chat returns the information about the train. Before departure, you can know the departure and arrival times and the scheduled platform. During the journey, passengers can receive updates via chat – also with push notifications, if active for the WhatsApp application – about travel times at stops and scheduled platforms, and the expected arrival time at the destination. ### Advantages for cross-border workers
The new Trenord ticket service on WhatsApp represents a significant advantage for cross-border workers who travel daily between Italy and the Canton Ticino. The ability to purchase tickets directly via WhatsApp greatly simplifies the purchasing process, eliminating the need to use the official Trenord app. This is particularly useful for those who are not used to using specific apps or who prefer the convenience of chat.

### Comparison with traditional tickets
Compared to traditional tickets, WhatsApp tickets offer several advantages. First, the convenience of having the ticket always at hand on your smartphone, without having to print it or keep it physically. Additionally, the ability to receive real-time updates about your trip via push notifications is an additional benefit, especially for frequent travelers who need to keep track of schedules and platforms.

### Step-by-step purchase procedure
1. Access self-service or Digital Gates: Go to the self-service or Digital Gates located at Milano Centrale, Milano Cadorna, Milano Porta Garibaldi, and Malpensa T1 stations.
2. **Select the WhatsApp option**: Select the WhatsApp option on the distributor's screen.
3. Scan the QR Code: Scan the QR Code displayed on the distributor's screen.
4. Generation of the pre-filled message: A pre-filled message will be generated in the 'Trenord Tickets' chat.
5. Confirm your phone number: Confirm the last digits of your phone number to receive the ticket link.
6. View the ticket: Open the link received in the chat to view the ticket and consult all details.
7. Activate the ticket: Activate the ticket via the dedicated function, if necessary.

### Final considerations
The new Trenord ticket service on WhatsApp represents a significant step forward in the digitalization of public transport services. The simplicity and practicality of this purchasing method make travel easier and more convenient for passengers, particularly for cross-border workers who need quick and efficient solutions for their daily commutes.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now
To start using the new Trenord ticket service on WhatsApp, simply go to the self-service machines or Digital Gates located at Milano Centrale, Milano Cadorna, Milano Porta Garibaldi, and Malpensa T1 stations. By following the described procedure, it will be possible to purchase tickets in a few minutes and always have all the necessary information for your journey at your fingertips.

### Useful tools
For further information and to plan your trips, you can use the [Trenord calculator](nav:calculator) available on the official website. Additionally, for those who wish to receive real-time updates on their train, you can use the 'My train' channel on WhatsApp.

### Conclusion
The Trenord ticket service on WhatsApp represents a significant innovation in the public transport sector, offering passengers a practical and convenient solution for purchasing tickets. For cross-border workers who travel daily between Italy and the Canton Ticino, this service represents an additional tool to simplify and optimize their movements. Use the [Trenord calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your trips and take advantage of all the features offered by this new service.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/il-biglietto-di-trenord-si-riceve-su-whatsapp/2560009/)*
